summary: Cathy O'Neil expresses enthusiasm about Citi Bike's expansion to the Columbia University area. The new bike stations will allow her to drop her kid off at school and bike downtown instead of taking the subway. She sees this as a major quality-of-life improvement, particularly avoiding the crowded 1 train during rush hour. She acknowledges with humor that the benefit depends on not everyone else having the same idea.
key_points: Citi Bike has expanded to the Columbia University neighborhood
The new stations enable a practical commute solution: school drop-off followed by biking downtown
This eliminates the need to take the crowded 1 train at rush hour
The benefit is contingent on adoption rates not overwhelming the system
money_quotes: I'm unreasonably excited that Citi Bike has finally expanded to the area where I live
People, this is huge. It means I never have to get on the 1 train at rush hour again!
Unless everyone else has the same plan as me, of course
key_insight: Small infrastructure expansions like bike-sharing can meaningfully improve daily urban life by providing alternatives to overcrowded public transit.
